summary.brief: Zaw v. Birusingh. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Zaw was referred by his primary care physician to The Iowa Clinic, P.C. for a circumcision.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At The Iowa Clinic, Zaw met with Dr. Kevin Birusingh. An interpreter hired by the clinic through LANGUAGEtech, Inc. attended the initial appointment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At the second appointment, at which Dr. Birusingh performed a vasectomy—not a circumcision—on Zaw.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Zaw and Siama were given a 'consent for sterilization' form, which included language about how it was the patient’s decision to be sterilized.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The form mentioned 'bilateral vasectomy' at least twice.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Zaw was given a 'No-Scalpel Vasectomy' brochure to take with him when he left.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury awarded Zaw $500,000 for past loss of bodily function; $250,000 for future loss of bodily function.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury awarded Zaw $1,000,000 for past physical and mental pain and suffering; and $250,000 for future physical and mental pain and suffering.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury concluded both that Dr. Birusingh was negligent and caused damage to Zaw and that Zaw was negligent or failed to mitigate his damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: It assigned 70% of the fault to Dr. Birusingh, and 30% to Zaw.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: REVERSED AND REMANDED FOR NEW TRIAL.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: Dr. Busky summarized his opinions during this exchange: Q: In terms of wrapping this up, insofar as your—any criticism that you intend to offer of Dr. Birusingh at trial in this matter.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: A. That’s fair. Q. And when you say he didn’t effectively communicate what he was going to do, that’s based on the fact— A. Yeah.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Because of the errors outlined above, we reverse the judgment and remand for a new trial on the informed-consent claim with all parties.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Immigrant from Burma, first language is Burmese

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports difficulty retracting foreskin and presence of a small bead under the skin on the dorsal shaft of the penis.

focused_exam.genitalia: Difficulty retracting foreskin, small bead under the skin on the dorsal shaft of the penis

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Phimosis with possible need for circumcision

requestables.name: Urology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with urologist for circumcision evaluation

reveal.result_summary: Patient seen for vasectomy consult instead of circumcision
reveal.decision_impact: Led to incorrect procedure being performed

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to primary care physician with urinary issues

timeline_phases.name: Urology Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient seen by urologist for consultation

key_events.event: Consultation for vasectomy instead of circumcision

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient referred for circumcision but seen for vasectomy consult
decision_points.prompt: What should the physician confirm before proceeding with the procedure?

options.description: Confirm the intended procedure with the patient using an interpreter
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures patient understanding and consent for the correct procedure

options.description: Proceed with vasectomy based on initial consultation notes
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumes patient understanding without direct confirmation

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with vasectomy without confirming intended procedure
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Assumed patient consent based on consultation notes

expected_action_court.description: Physician should have confirmed the intended procedure with the patient
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony emphasized the need for clear communication and confirmation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to confirm the intended procedure led to performing an unwanted vasectomy
explanation.what_should_have_happened: The physician should have used an interpreter to confirm the patient's understanding and consent for the correct procedure
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care requires clear communication and informed consent

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent vasectomy instead of circumcision
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of bodily function related to sterilization

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to obtain informed consent for the correct procedure

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to confirm and perform the correct procedure
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper confirmation would have led to circumcision

factual_timeline.event: Consultation for vasectomy instead of circumcision

allegations.allegation: Failure to obtain informed consent for the correct procedure

court_findings.legal_significance: Performed incorrect procedure without informed consent
